oai_response.prompt:
Here is code in Python 3 programming lanaguge. Translate the following code from Python 3 to C programming lanaguge. Do not output any extra description or tokens other than the translated code. 

def lint(n):    #to find length of an integer
    h = int(n)
    i= 0
    while(h>0):
          h= int(h/10)
          i=i+1
    len =i
    return len


k =int(input())
list = [1]
n=1
y=1
for y in range (1,k):
        n = n+1;
        p= lint(n)
        for i in range(p,0,-1):
                e= 10**(i-1)
                x = int(n/e)
                z= x%10 
                list.append(z)


        y = y+p
print(list[k-1])

||END-of-SRC||

source_data.prob_desc_input_spec: The first and only line contains integer $$$k$$$ ($$$1 \le k \le 10000$$$) — the position to process ($$$1$$$-based index).
source_data.source_code:
def lint(n):    #to find length of an integer
    h = int(n)
    i= 0
    while(h>0):
          h= int(h/10)
          i=i+1
    len =i
    return len


k =int(input())
list = [1]
n=1
y=1
for y in range (1,k):
        n = n+1;
        p= lint(n)
        for i in range(p,0,-1):
                e= 10**(i-1)
                x = int(n/e)
                z= x%10 
                list.append(z)


        y = y+p
print(list[k-1])
source_data.prob_desc_output_spec: Print the $$$k$$$-th digit of the resulting infinite sequence.
source_data.prob_desc_description: Let's write all the positive integer numbers one after another from $$$1$$$ without any delimiters (i.e. as a single string). It will be the infinite sequence starting with 123456789101112131415161718192021222324252627282930313233343536...Your task is to print the $$$k$$$-th digit of this sequence.
